versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: “Hamath and Arpad are put to shame,
for they have heard a bad report;
they are agitated like the sea;
anguish and pain have seized her
25 How is the city of praise not forsaken,
the town that brings Me joy?
26 For her young men will fall in the streets,
and all her warriors will be silenced in that day,”
27 “I will set fire to the walls of Damascus;
it will consume the fortresses of Ben-hadad.”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 49**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Judgment on Damascus** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 49** remains the first control on meaning.
